title: ranunculus peltatus subsp. fucoides
intro: Ranunculus peltatus subsp. fucoides, also known as Water Crowsfoot, is a subspecies of aquatic plant belonging to the Ranunculaceae family, characterized by its distinctive, cleft leaves and white or pinkish flowers. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, primarily for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The medicinal properties of Ranunculus peltatus subsp. fucoides have been reported to offer various health benefits, including relief from skin irritations, wound healing, and anti-cancer properties. The plant can be prepared as an infusion, decoction, or topical cream, making it a versatile herbal remedy for various health conditions.
